books.title: Making of Canada: An Epic History in Twenty Extraordinary Lives
books.author: Greg Koabel
books.rationale: In this engaging book, Greg Koabel challenges the notion that pre-Confederation Canada unfolded in a linear fashion and instead suggests that nation-building involved greater "regional, national, and human diversity" than is typically acknowledged. To illustrate this argument, Koabel introduces his readers to twenty episodes significant to Canada's history but whose major players have been largely forgotten. These biographical vignettes give readers a more personal - and memorable - connection to Canadian history.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are self-contained.

books.title: A History of Canada in Ten Maps: Epic Stories of Charting a Mysterious Land
books.author: Adam Shoalts
books.rationale: This book tells the story of the discovery and development of the land that would become Canada by exploring the creation and usage of ten significant maps. It is an innovative and engaging approach to Canadian history, written by an academic and modern-day adventurer who has followed many of the routes taken by the explorers he includes in this book. It shows how history writing can create a more vivid representation of the past when it draws on biography, story-telling, geography, and cartography.
books.noteText: This book will be used over two years in high school (Rotation 2 and Rotation 3). In 2026-2027, students will read the second half of the book. Interested students may wish to read the first half over the summer, but it is not necessary since all chapters are mostly self-contained.

books.title: Early Voices
books.author: Mary Alice Downie
books.rationale: This collection of writings by 29 different women presents a kaleidoscope image of Canada's history. The wide range of individual voices, backgrounds, and histories provided by these primary documents complement the other historical non-fiction and fiction read during the year.
books.noteText: This book will be used over multiple years during high school
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 11-12
Canadian History: Grades 9-10

books.title: How Different It Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation
books.author: Michael J. Goodspeed
books.rationale: One of the key lessons of history is that the past was - to greater or lesser degrees - fundamentally different than the present, and this is shown clearly in Michael Goodspeed's book, How Different it Was: Canadians at the Time of Confederation. Goodspeed provides a multi-faceted account of the experiences of Canadians in the 1860s, exploring factors such as geographical differences, cultural practices, family dynamics, religious observances, scientific beliefs, leisure activities, habits, and attitudes, as well as the burgeoning nation of Canada's place in the wider nineteenth-century geopolitical arena. He draws heavily on primary source material, and the book is full of historical photographs and documents.

books.title: The Unweaving
books.author: Cheryl Perisien
books.rationale: The Red River Resistance of 1869 (also known as the First Riel Rebellion) is brought to life through the experiences of one Métis family in this work of historical fiction. Unfolding in four perspectives, this novel explores how the turmoil and the conflict affect each member of the family differently. The narrative weaves historical events and people (including the author's own ancestor) into the lives of this one family, and it is a thoroughly researched, richly detailed, and beautifully written account of the great upheaval faced by the Red River Colony in the years after Confederation.

books.title: When America First Met China
books.author: Eric Jay Dolin
books.rationale: This is the story of trade between nations. One nation has just been born from a bloody battle with Britain, and one nation is steeped in a 2000-year tradition. The United States of America has to break away from using its mother nation for trading, and as it navigates trade with China, there will be collisions, barriers, and failings, but there will also be triumphs. China’s culture is also explored in great depth as Dolin uses stories of real people who broke the rules and recounted all the different happenings taking place in this nation.

books.title: Erebus: The Story of a Ship
books.author: Michael Palin
books.rationale: Erebus is a book about exploration and mystery. Palin takes the student into two of the most harrowing exploration trips ever recorded, the last trip being unsuccessful and leading to a mystery that still puzzles modern-day minds. By reading Erebus, students will get an account of what is known about the first ever explorations into Antarctica, the most isolated continent in the world.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grade 9
World History: Grades 10-12

books.title: Blood and Iron: The Rise and Fall of the German Empire
books.author: Katja Hoyer
books.rationale: Katja Hoyer is a German-British historian and expert in the field of Germany’s history. She brings that expertise to the pages of her book which engages with the people, policies, and places that helped build the German Empire and ultimately led to its downfall. This book offers the student the history of a nation whose national pride led to two World Wars.
books.sharedText:
World History: Grades 10-12
World History: Grade 9

books.title: Christians Reading Classics
books.author: Nadya Williams
books.rationale: Christianity began in a pagan land with its own myths and writings about gods, villains, and heroes. These stories would become ingrained in the culture. Stories from Homer and writings from Plato and Aristotle would impact the Greco-Roman world and still impact our world.  Williams explores the importance of understanding the content of these classics and the way they impacted the world, as Christians.
books.noteText: Only specific chapters will be assigned.

books.title: Ancient Empires: From Mesopotamia to the Rise of Islam
books.author: Eric H. Cline & Mark W. Graham
books.rationale: Ancient Empires is a relatively brief yet comprehensive and even-handed overview of the ancient Near East, the Mediterranean, and Europe, including the Greco-Roman world, Late Antiquity, and the early Muslim period.

books.title: Early Christian Writings: The Apostolic Fathers edited
books.author: Andrew Louth
books.rationale: With the birth of Christianity came a lot of writings from a lot of different authors. In this collection, authors like Clement, Ignatius, Barnabas, and Polycarp contribute to the growth of Christianity and write documents against heresies, trying to answer questions of philosophy and questions about their God.
